stack_spec.name: Spec4
languages: JavaScript (inline/embedded in Dash clientside callbacks only)

web_framework_and_ui.name: dash
web_framework_and_ui.purpose: Core web framework — Flask-based reactive UI via callbacks, dcc.Store for client-side credential storage in browser localStorage

web_framework_and_ui.name: dash-mantine-components
web_framework_and_ui.purpose: React/Mantine-based UI component library — rich, polished components with theming support

web_framework_and_ui.name: dash-iconify
web_framework_and_ui.purpose: Icon integration for Dash via the Iconify icon set ecosystem

llm_abstraction.name: litellm
llm_abstraction.purpose: Unified multi-provider LLM abstraction — single API for OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Cohere, Mistral and 100+ other providers; handles streaming, image inputs, and model capability metadata

web_search_integration.name: mcp
web_search_integration.purpose: MCP Python SDK — integrates Tavily web search tool into all five agents via the Model Context Protocol

wsgi_server.name: gunicorn
wsgi_server.purpose: Production WSGI server for serving the Dash/Flask app when run locally after PyPI install

testing.name: pytest
testing.purpose: Test runner — simple, expressive, widely adopted Python testing framework

testing.name: pytest-cov
testing.purpose: Test coverage reporting integrated with pytest

quality_tooling.name: ruff
quality_tooling.purpose: Linter and formatter — replaces Flake8 and Black in a single tool

quality_tooling.name: mypy
quality_tooling.purpose: Static type checker in strict mode — enforces full annotation coverage across the codebase

coding_style.linter: Ruff (rule sets E and F; E501 suppressed per-file for agents/*.py and app.py where long strings are unavoidable)
other_rules: from __future__ import annotations in all agent modules for deferred annotation evaluation
patterns: layered architecture (presentation / business logic / session & persistence)
sequential pipeline (Reviewer → Brainstormer → Designer → StackAdvisor → Phaser) — each stage consumes prior output as input
functional core / imperative shell — pure functions for data transformation and agent logic; side effects (filesystem I/O, LLM calls, HTTP) pushed to the edges
dependency injection — agents receive dependencies (LLM client, session state, filesystem paths) rather than constructing them internally
single underscore prefix for private helpers at both function and module level
